steps.title: Find the slope from the two given points
steps.content:
The company's profit is \$170 when selling 4 items and \$590 when selling 10 items. These give us two points: $(4, 170)$ and $(10, 590)$.

The slope of a linear function is:
$$m = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1} = \frac{590 - 170}{10 - 4} = \frac{420}{6} = 70$$

steps.title: Find the y-intercept and identify the equation
steps.content:
Now use the point-slope form with one of the points. Using $(4, 170)$ and slope $m = 70$:
$$f(x) = 70x + b$$
$$170 = 70(4) + b$$
$$170 = 280 + b$$
$$b = -110$$

Therefore $f(x) = 70x - 110$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
